title: Tallon split
content:
{{Top|leg}}
{{Quote|A Tallon split? But that's just a hotshot training maneuver.|Derek Klivian expressing his skepticism of the maneuver before successfully performing it himself during the Battle of Hoth|<ref name="Running the Gauntlet-GG3-2">{{WEGCite|story=Running the Gauntlet|book=Galaxy Guide 3: The Empire Strikes Back|reprint=1}}</ref>}}
The '''Tallon split''' was a risky tactical maneuver.

==Background==
The maneuver was theoretically simple, but took a great amount of skill, precision, and concentration to execute correctly. When applied successfully, the Tallon split would allow two [[Starfighter/Legends|starfighters]] to inflict a considerable amount of damage on an unsuspecting [[Capital ship/Legends|capital ship]]. The tactic called for two starfighters to fly extremely close together as they approached a capital ship, so close that the larger [[Starship/Legends|ship's]] [[Targeting computer/Legends|targeting computer]] would detect the two fighters as single approaching ship. At very close range, the two fighters would split apart: one fighter would continue on a strafing run of the capital ship, while the second would draw fire away from the first. The attacking fighter would have about five seconds to cause damage on the capital ship before it was clearly identified as a second ship. Even so, those five seconds would allow enough time to cause serious damage to the larger vessel.<ref name="Running the Gauntlet-GG3-2" />

==History==
The tactic was executed with surprising success during the [[Battle of Hoth/Legends|evacuation]] of [[Hoth/Legends|Hoth]] in [[3 ABY/Legends|3 ABY]] by Rebel starfighters flying escort for [[GR-75 medium transport/Legends|GR-75 medium transports]] attempting to run the Imperial blockade of the [[Planet/Legends|planet]]. Most notably, [[Rogue Squadron/Legends|Rogue Squadron]] pilots [[Wedge Antilles/Legends|Wedge Antilles]], [[Derek Klivian/Legends|Derek Klivian]], [[Wes Janson/Legends|Wes Janson]], and [[Luke Skywalker/Legends|Luke Skywalker]] employed the tactic to help the transport [[Thon's Orchard/Legends|''Thon's Orchard'']] safely reach [[Hyperspace/Legends|hyperspace]]. Antilles piloted his [[T-65 X-wing starfighter/Legends|T-65 X-wing starfighter]] close to Klivian and Janson in their [[BTL-S3 Y-wing starfighter/Legends|BTL-S3 Y-wing starfighter]] as "one target," as they attacked an [[Imperial-class Star Destroyer/Legends|''Imperial''-class Star Destroyer]] blocking the transport's flight path. In conjunction with those two ships, Skywalker also attacked from a hidden location flying underneath the transport. The Star Destroyer was distracted and damaged enough to allow the ''Thon's Orchard'' to reach safety.<ref name="Running the Gauntlet-GG3-2" /><ref name="The Essential Guide to Characters">''[[The Essential Guide to Characters]]''</ref>

==Behind the scenes==
The name of this maneuver corresponds to [[Adar Tallon/Legends|Adar Tallon]], a Rebel Alliance starfighter tactician. However, no source explicitly attributes the tactic to Tallon's design.

==Sources==
*<!-- 1989-XX-XX -->''[[Galaxy Guide 3: The Empire Strikes Back]]''
*<!-- 1993-09-XX -->''[[The Movie Trilogy Sourcebook]]''
*<!-- 1995-10-XX -->''[[The Essential Guide to Characters]]''
